narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Los Angeles Mayor Eric Garcetti joined a “global coalition of mayors” at a summit on climate change this week in Buenos Aires, Argentina, burning fossil fuels on an international flight rather than attending remotely and attending to pressing issues at home.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes the mayor's decision to travel to a climate summit by plane, questioning the effectiveness and logic of the action by highlighting the contradiction between the stated goal (fighting climate change) and the means (burning fossil fuels).

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: White Garcetti was away, Los Angeles continued to struggle with the high crime and homelessness that have come to define his legacy, as well as a crisis in the City Council triggered by racist comments from City Council members within Garcetti’s party.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement directly criticizes Mayor Garcetti's competence and priorities by contrasting his international trip with pressing domestic problems (crime, homelessness, political crisis) that define his tenure, challenging his effectiveness as an authority.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: It is not clear why the conference could not be held remotely.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement implicitly challenges the motives and integrity of the climate movement (represented by the C40 summit), suggesting its actions (holding an in-person conference requiring air travel) are hypocritical or not genuinely focused on solving the problem.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: burning fossil fuels on an international flight rather than attending remotely and attending to pressing issues at home.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The quote directly questions the effectiveness and consistency of the mayor's climate action by highlighting the carbon cost of his travel and suggesting he should have prioritized local problems, which fits the definition of criticizing the motives and impact of climate policies.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: though L.A.’s infrastructure is not widely considered “high-quality.” A proposed high-speed rail between L.A. and San Francisco has been largely canceled, and current passenger rail service from L.A. to San Diego has been suspended because of a potential landslide hazard.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement challenges the competence and credibility of local government institutions (specifically transportation infrastructure under the mayor's purview) in the context of their climate commitments, aligning with the narrative of criticizing authorities.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Garcetti’s “Green New Deal” calls for shuttering natural gas plants; it has been opposed by public works unions, and lost the only serious electoral challenge it faced in a 2019 city council race.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The quote presents the climate policy (Green New Deal) as facing opposition and electoral failure, which challenges the movement's political support and practical impact, fitting the narrative of criticizing the climate movement's effectiveness and acceptance.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: That is one-fourth (economy) or one-half (premium) the global average per capita use of carbon per year. It is not clear why the conference could not be held remotely.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: By quantifying the flight's emissions as a fraction of an annual average and questioning the necessity of in-person attendance, the text minimizes the significance of the individual action in the broader context of climate change, which aligns with downplaying its urgency or impact.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: burning fossil fuels on an international flight rather than attending remotely and attending to pressing issues at home.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text directly questions the effectiveness and motives of Garcetti's climate action by highlighting the contradiction between his stated goal and the carbon-intensive method of travel, implying the policy action is hypocritical or ineffective.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: though L.A.’s infrastructure is not widely considered “high-quality.” A proposed high-speed rail between L.A. and San Francisco has been largely canceled, and current passenger rail service from L.A. to San Diego has been suspended...
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote challenges the competence and integrity of Mayor Garcetti and his administration by pointing out the failure of local transportation projects, undermining his authority to speak on 'high-quality urban transportation infrastructure' at an international summit.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: White Garcetti was away, Los Angeles continued to struggle with the high crime and homelessness that have come to define his legacy, as well as a crisis in the City Council...
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement criticizes the climate movement and its representatives by suggesting that participation in international climate summits is a distraction from pressing domestic issues, thereby challenging the movement's priorities and impact.
